name: Colorado Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Colorado - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Division of Professions and Occupations — Speech-Language Pathology

address.description:
Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies
Division of Professions and Occupations
1560 Broadway, Suite 1350
Denver, CO 80202

requiredDocuments.name: Praxis exam score report
requiredDocuments.description: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Colorado SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application and license fee is $120. Biennial renewal is $75.

mainEntity.name: Is Colorado part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Colorado has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

mainEntity.name: Does Colorado allow telepractice for SLPs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Colorado permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.

mainEntity.name: Does Colorado have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Colorado participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Colorado without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Colorado?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Colorado while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
